title: Inaugural Nascent Research Ideas
description_html:
Anelise Assumpção will discuss her doctoral research, and the complexity of dealing with data in text format that requires a high degree of interpretation. How the inherent complexity of textual data arises from its dependence on interpretation, context, and linguistic nuance, which makes its processing particularly challenging in academic research.

Emilio Munoz will present his work on Order-Aware State Representations for Path-Dependent Reinforcement Learning, exploring how we might design compact state representations that preserve chronological order while still being computationally feasible.

Phileas Dazeley-Gaist will discuss their ongoing work with the 2025 CGS Sympoietic Art Organism group. The talk will showcase a citation network grown by a small artist community in intentional collaboration working from a shared commons. Over a series of co-creative cycles, artists iteratively remix and take inspiration from works already in the commons, contributing new pieces while tracking citation relationships.

title: Scaling Laws in Urban Environments
description_html: Dr. Ana Petrova explored the fascinating world of scaling laws in cities, from infrastructure needs to economic output. This talk provided a deep dive into the mathematical principles that govern urban systems.

title: Rapid-Fire: AI & Emergent Behavior
description_html: Three researchers presented their work on emergent behaviors in large-scale AI models. Topics included spontaneous cooperation, unintended capabilities, and the challenges of alignment.

title: The Emergence of Collective Intelligence
description_html:
Join us for an in-depth lecture from Dr. Elara Vance on the foundational principles of collective intelligence. We will explore how simple interactions between agents can lead to complex, adaptive group behaviors.

This talk will cover examples from social insects, bird flocks, and human organizations, followed by an extended Q&A session.

title: Rapid-Fire: Network Models & Urban Systems
description_html:
Our Nascent Research series returns with three rapid-fire presentations on the application of network models to urban systems. Each speaker will have 10 minutes to present their emerging research, followed by 10 minutes of Q&A.

This session is perfect for sparking new collaborations and getting a quick overview of the latest developments in the field.

title: Intro to Agent-Based Modeling
description_html:
Join us for a hands-on workshop designed to introduce the fundamentals of agent-based modeling (ABM). This session will cover basic concepts and guide participants through building their first models in NetLogo.

No prior experience is required.

title: Advanced Network Analysis in Python
description_html:
Go beyond the basics in this hands-on workshop. Dr. Tanaka will guide participants through advanced techniques in network analysis using Python libraries like NetworkX and igraph.

Prerequisites: Basic Python knowledge and familiarity with Jupyter notebooks.

title: Antifragility: Beyond Robustness
description_html: This talk explores the concept of antifragility, where systems gain from disorder, volatility, and stressors. Dr. Hamid will discuss how this principle applies to everything from financial markets to biological systems.

title: The Dynamics of Information Spread
description_html: This session covered how information and misinformation spread through social networks. Dr. Kenji Tanaka presented models that help us understand and predict viral phenomena online.

title: The Future of Urban Complexity
description_html: A recording of our inaugural talk. Prof. Maria Flores discusses the challenges and opportunities for cities in the 21st century, viewed through the lens of complexity science.

title: Rapid-Fire: Econophysics and Market Dynamics
description_html: A look back at our session on econophysics. Presenters shared rapid-fire talks on market volatility, agent-based financial models, and network effects in economic systems.

title: Rapid-Fire: Evolvability and ALife
description_html: Join us for three exciting 10-minute talks on the frontiers of evolvability and artificial life. This session will explore how complex systems learn, adapt, and innovate.

title: Genetic Algorithms: From Theory to Practice
description_html:
(Coming Soon) A hands-on workshop on the principles and applications of genetic algorithms. Learn how to implement GAs to solve optimization and search problems.

Registration details will be posted in January.
